results.title: 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters
results.snippet: # 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters. These rates indicate the confidence of investors in the economy. * The 10-year yield is used as a proxy for mortgage rates and is also seen as a sign of investor sentiment about the economy. The 10-year Treasury note yield is commonly used as a measure of the U.S. government’s 10-year borrowing rate. It serves as a benchmark for other interest rates and is a key indicator of investor sentiment about economic conditions. ## Understanding 10-Year Treasury Bond Yields. The U.S Treasury sells bonds via auction, and yields are set through a bidding process.3 Prices for the 10-year bond drop when confidence is high, which causes yields to rise. results.title: The 10-year Treasury yield is moving the 'wrong way' for stocks
results.snippet: 2. Best high-yield savings accounts. 3. Best money market accounts. # The 10-year Treasury yield is moving the 'wrong way' for stocks. **What happened:** The 10-year yield (^TNX) rose about 6 basis points to 4.68% Tuesday, while the 30-year yield (^TYX) rose 5 basis points to 5.2%, hitting its highest level since July 2007. As Yahoo Finance's Jared Blikre has written, yields on both the 10-year and 30-year Treasurys are testing key psychological levels for investors, who are weighing the impact of inflation from the surge in oil prices on global growth. "I think the simple thing is that if rates don't go down here, the [price-to-earnings] multiple [on the equal-weighted S&P 500] is not going to rebound higher, and it's going to get more difficult and challenging for equities to make gains,
